figures.caption: Figure 1: Joint distributions of N1 and R = N2/N1. High-R ridges at multiple integer supports reveal higherorder entropy peaks across datasets and model families.

Figure 2: Occurrence of plateau-k states, defined by N1 ∈ [k − 0.3, k + 0.3] and R > 0.95. Instruction tuning increases low-order plateaus (k = 1–5) while reducing or preserving higher-order plateaus (k > 5).

Table 1: Fine-tuning results on the mixed-domain dataset. Generation tasks are reported as pass@1 / pass@k; MMLU is 5-shot accuracy. Avg. is the mean of all displayed scores in each row. Bold numbers indicate the best result among fine-tuned methods, excluding the base model.
Figure 3: Qwen3-14B fine-tuning results across three training datasets.
Table 2: Fine-tuning results on UltraFeedback. Generation tasks are reported as pass@1 / pass@k; MMLU is 5-shot accuracy. Avg. is the mean of all displayed scores in each row.
Table 3: Top-10 Prediction Distribution for Instance 1 (k = 2).
Table 4: Top-10 Prediction Distribution for Instance 2 (k = 3).
Table 5: Top-10 Prediction Distribution for Instance 3 (k = 4).
Table 12: Top-20 Prediction Distribution for Instance 10 (k = 12).
Table 15: Fine-tuning results on Magicoder-OSS-Instruct-75K. Generation tasks are reported as pass@1 / pass@k; MMLU is 5-shot accuracy. Avg. is the mean of all displayed scores in each row.

Figure 4: Sensitivity to the preservation weight µ. Results are obtained with Qwen3-4B fine-tuned on UltraFeedback and evaluated on MATH-500, MBPP+, and MMLU. Performance varies only slightly across µ ∈ [0.1, 1.0], suggesting that LP-SFT is relatively insensitive to the choice of µ.
Table 16: Fine-tuning results on NuminaMath-CoT. Generation tasks are reported as pass@1 / pass@k; MMLU is 5-shot accuracy. Avg. is the mean of all displayed scores in each row.

Table 17: Ablation results for LP-SFT design choices on Qwen3-4B trained with UltraFeedback. We ablate three design factors: removing the supervised token yt from the preservation set At, using normalized probabilities to compute the KL loss, and applying the KL loss over either a local preservation set or the full vocabulary. Generation tasks are reported as pass@1 / pass@k; MMLU is 5-shot accuracy. Avg. is the mean of all displayed scores in each row.

Table 18: Support-schedule comparison on UltraFeedback. We compare the default N1-adaptive schedule with a fixed top-10 schedule across three backbone models. Generation tasks are reported as pass@1 / pass@k; MMLU is 5-shot accuracy. Avg. is the mean of all displayed scores in each row.
